sections.paragraph:
ἀπρεπής, ές (s. πρέπει) lit. ‘not fitting’ pert. to not meeting a standard and therefore subject to rejection, unsuitable (fig. Thu.; Pla., Laws 788b; Epicrates Com. 11, 33 Edmonds; Lucian, Dial. Deor. 13, 1; Aelian, VH 14, 19; PTebt 765, 4; 802, 15; PAmh 142, 8; 4 Macc 6:17; Philo, Cher. 92; Jos., Ant. 18, 314, Vi. 146) lit. (Artem. 2, 3 p. 88, 6 ἀ. ἐσθῆτες) λίθοι ἀ. ἐν τ. οἰκοδομῇ stones that are not suitable for the building Hs 9, 4, 6f.—DELG s.v. πρέπω.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 125
